title: National Chambal Sanctuary
tagline: National Chambal Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: National Chambal Sanctuary, also called the National Chambal Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary, is a 5,400 km2 (2,100 sq mi) tri-state protected area in northern India for the protection
overview.description: National Chambal Sanctuary, also called the National Chambal Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary, is a 5,400 km2 (2,100 sq mi) tri-state protected area in northern India for the protection of the Critically Endangered gharial, the red-crowned roof turtle and the Endangered Ganges river dolphin. Located on the Chambal River near the tripoint of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh, it was first declared in Madhya Pradesh in 1978, and now constitutes a long narrow eco-reserve co-administered by the three states. topPlaces.name: Ater Fort
topPlaces.description: Ater Fort is a fort located 35 km (22 mi) west of Bhind in Bhind district, Madhya Pradesh, India. It was built by Bhadauria kings Badan Singh, Maha Singh and Bakhat Singh between 1664 and 1668.

topPlaces.name: Sarokhipura
topPlaces.description: Sarokhipura is a village in Panchayat Mukutpura of Jaitpur Kalan Block at Bah Tehsil of Agra, Uttar Pradesh, India India. It is situated on Fatehabad road 85 km from Agra City.

items.note: Ater Fort is a fort located 35 km (22 mi) west of Bhind in Bhind district, Madhya Pradesh, India. It was built by Bhadauria kings Badan Singh, Maha Singh and Bakhat Singh between 1664 and 1668.
Sarokhipura is a village in Panchayat Mukutpura of Jaitpur Kalan Block at Bah Tehsil of Agra, Uttar Pradesh, India India. It is situated on Fatehabad road 85 km from Agra City.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit National Chambal Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit National Chambal Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach National Chambal Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gwalior Airport (67 km) and the nearest railway station is Gwalior Junction (77 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for National Chambal Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of National Chambal Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in National Chambal Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in National Chambal Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in National Chambal Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ater Fort, Sarokhipura.
